sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The top charcoal is moving left and growing! It started at cols 31-63 (33px) and is now at cols 18-63 (46px). Each click moves it left by 1-2 columns and adds 1-2 pixels. It seems to be "painting" as it moves left.
The top charcoal is still moving left after 10 clicks. Let me continue moving it left to see where it stops.
Actually, looking at the pattern:
- Click 1: cols 29-63 (35px) - moved 2 cols left, +2px
- Click 2: cols 28-63 (36px) - moved 1 col left, +1px
- Click 3: cols 27-63 (37px) - moved 1 col left, +1px
- Click 4: cols 26-63 (38px) - moved 1 col left, +1px
- Click 5: cols 24-63 (40px) - moved 2 cols left, +2px
- Click 6: cols 23-63 (41px) - moved 1 col left, +1px
- Click 7: cols 22-63 (42px) - moved 1 col left, +1px
- Click 8: cols 20-63 (44px) - moved 2 cols left, +2px
- Click 9: cols 19-63 (45px) - moved 1 col left, +1px
- Click 10: cols 18-63 (46px) - moved 1 col left, +1px
The pattern seems to be: 2, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1... So every 4th click moves 2 columns, and the others move 1 column. This is similar to the bottom charcoal's movement pattern.
The top charcoal is still moving left. Let me continue to see where it stops.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Continue moving top charcoal left
print("Continuing to move top charcoal left...")
for i in range(20):
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 18, 'col': 2}])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] > 20:
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            print(f"  After click {i+11}: top charcoal cols = {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done'):
        print("  Level completed!")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
